prerequisites: A Polygon.io account (Polygon.io now operates under the Massive brand at massive.com); create one to obtain an API key.
An active API key generated from the account dashboard, supplied as the apiKey config field.
A stock ticker symbol to pull (stocksTicker, case-sensitive, e.g. AAPL).
A bar/aggregate window defined by multiplier and timespan (e.g. multiplier=1, timespan=day).
A date range to query, set with start_date and end_date (YYYY-MM-DD).

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a single Polygon.io (Massive) API key, mapped to the apiKey secret field. Polygon.io accepts the key either as the apiKey query parameter on the request URL or via the Authorization header. No OAuth or client credentials are used.

setupSteps.title: Create an account and generate an API key
setupSteps.body: Sign up for a Polygon.io account at the dashboard (now hosted at massive.com/dashboard/signup). After signing in, open the API keys section of the dashboard to create or copy your API key. Treat the key as a secret and do not commit it to source control.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the apiKey field (the connector's secret field) to the key from your dashboard. Polygon.io accepts this key as an apiKey query parameter or in the Authorization header; the connector handles transmitting it.

setupSteps.title: Choose the ticker and aggregate window
setupSteps.body: Set stocksTicker to the case-sensitive symbol you want (for example AAPL). Set multiplier and timespan to define each bar; these combine into the aggregates path GET /v2/aggs/ticker/{stocksTicker}/range/{multiplier}/{timespan}/{from}/{to}. For example multiplier=1 and timespan=day yields daily bars.

setupSteps.title: Set the date range
setupSteps.body: Set start_date and end_date (the from and to bounds of the aggregates endpoint) in YYYY-MM-DD format to bound the historical window you extract.

setupSteps.title: Optionally tune adjusted, sort, and limit
setupSteps.body: Set adjusted to control whether results are adjusted for stock splits (defaults to true). Set sort to order results by timestamp (asc for ascending). Set limit to cap the number of aggregates returned (up to 50,000; default 5,000).
